What You’ll Learn

Our Curriculum

A structured, personal path tailored to where you are right now.

بِسْمِ اللّٰهِ
Beginners

Quran Reading Foundation

Arabic alphabet to fluent Quran reading — from absolute zero, step by step.

Beginners

Quran Reading Foundation

  • Arabic alphabet & pronunciation
  • Joining letters and forming words
  • Vowel marks (Harakaat) mastery
  • Basic Tajweed rules introduced gradually
  • Short Surahs and daily prayers
  • Building reading fluency and confidence
۞
Hifz Program

Quran Memorisation

Memorize the Quran at your own pace with a personal daily plan and revision cycles.

Hifz Program

Quran Memorisation

  • Personalised Hifz plan at your pace
  • Daily new lesson + same-day revision
  • Weekly and monthly revision cycles
  • Teacher tracks every Juz personally
  • Memorisation techniques and motivation
  • Complete Hifz with strong long-term retention
ﻵ
Tajweed

Rules of Recitation

Master the science of proper Quran pronunciation and beautiful recitation.

Tajweed

Rules of Recitation

  • Makharij al-Huroof (articulation points)
  • Sifaat al-Huroof (letter characteristics)
  • Noon & Meem Sakinah rules
  • Madd (elongation) rules
  • Waqf & Ibtida (stopping & starting)
  • Advanced intermediate Tajweed rulings
★
Advanced

Revision & Strengthening

For Huffaz — strengthen your memorization with long-term retention plans.

Advanced

Revision & Strengthening

  • Full Quran revision for Huffaz
  • Speed and fluency improvement
  • Granular Tajweed correction sessions
  • Advanced recitation refinement
  • One-to-one teacher recitation review
  • Long-term Hifz protection plan

Begin the Journey

About Us

Al Kanz Academy

Al Kanz — meaning “The Treasure” — was founded on one belief: that the Quran belongs to everyone. No one should be held back by age, schedule, or circumstance.

Our teachers don’t just teach — they guide, motivate, and walk alongside every student on their journey.

We run one-to-one classes only. No batches. No shortcuts. Just real, personal attention.

FAQ

Common
Questions

Can't find your answer?

Contact us →

  • Do I need prior knowledge?

    No prior knowledge is required. Our teachers are experienced in teaching complete beginners, children and adults alike. We start from the very basics — Arabic letters, pronunciation, and Tajweed rules — tailored to your level.

  • How young can students start?

    Students can start as young as 4–5 years old. Our teachers are trained in child-friendly methods, making learning the Quran an enjoyable and memorable experience from an early age.

  • What platform do you use?

    We conduct all classes via Google Meet, making it easy to join from anywhere in the world. All you need is a stable internet connection and a device with a camera and microphone.

  • Can I choose my timings?

    Yes. We offer flexible scheduling 7 days a week, with morning, afternoon, and evening slots available to suit your timezone. Simply let us know your preference when you register.

  • Are there classes for sisters?

    Absolutely. We have dedicated female teachers available for sisters and young girls who prefer to learn with a female instructor in a comfortable, private environment.

  • How long is each session?

    Standard sessions are 30 minutes, 3 days a week. We can adjust based on your learning goals — some students prefer longer sessions for deeper practice.

  • What is Hifz?

    Hifz refers to the memorisation of the Holy Quran in its entirety. A student who completes this journey is called a Hafiz (male) or Hafiza (female) — one who has preserved the Quran in their heart.

  • How do I get started?

    Getting started is simple. Message us on WhatsApp, choose a convenient time, and meet your teacher for a free trial class. No commitment required — just come as you are.
